title: Phyteuma supina
taxonomy: Phyteuma supina is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Campanulaceae. It belongs to the genus Phyteuma, which consists of around seven species of plants native to Europe and Asia. The Campanulaceae family is a group of flowering plants commonly known as bellflowers. intro: Phyteuma supina, also known as woodland rattlesnake root, is a perennial herbaceous plant belonging to the Campanulaceae family. The plant has been traditionally used in medicine due to its various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which can help alleviate symptoms of digestive issues, respiratory problems, and skin conditions. The bioactive constituents of Phyteuma supina include sesquiterpenes, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties.
